meaning: n.缰绳,统治,支配vt.以缰绳控制,统治,驾驭vi.勒住马,止住
examples: Many people have begun looking for long-term ways to rein in spending.
Her parents kept her on a tight rein with their narrow and inflexible views.
He promised that between now and the end of the year the government would try to rein back inflation.
The government must get its finances in order and, in particular, rein in spending.
But with this sprawl came incredible complexity, which managers did very little to rein in.
